title: SharkTank 2.0
name: Research and Development Club

overview.description: A premier pitching competition where participants present innovative ideas, projects, or prototypes to “Sharks,” fostering entrepreneurship by linking academic research with real world commercialization.

rules: Teams of 2–4 (individuals allowed) present original, non-commercialized ideas.
Presentations must be in professional/business casual attire and last 8 minutes including Q&A.

title: Bomb's Away!
name: Robotics Club

overview.description: Bomb’s Away challenges participants to time the release of a payload from a remotely piloted aircraft onto a ground target. Success depends on judging speed, altitude, and wind for maximum accuracy.

rules: Pilot announces the approach, participant times the trigger to drop the payload, and marshals record the result.
No communication or interference with the pilot is allowed during the run.

title: Blaze
name: Robotics Club

overview.description: BLAZE is a laser Tag experience featuring a custom-built arena and DIY technology, challenging two teams to a 15-minute survivor match.

rules: Two teams of four compete for 15 minutes; winners are decided by points or full elimination.
Players have 3 lives and register via RFID or manually; scores are tracked live.

title: Sumo War
name: Robotics Club

overview.description: Enter the arena for an electrifying Sumo bot battle where teams fight to dominate the warzone. Push, punch, and strategize to outsmart opponents and claim victory!

rules: Two teams with 2 Sumobots each compete for 5 minutes, winning by pushing opponents out.
If time ends, points (+10 Danger Zone, +5 punch) determine the winner.

title: Cross The Rope
name: SEA Club

overview.description: 'CROSS THE ROPE' challenges participants to design and build an efficient rope-climbing robot using a basic chassis and selected components.

rules: Use provided components to customize gripping mechanisms and control algorithms.
Complete assembly and programming within time and pass all safety checks.

title: Project Showcase
name: SEA Club

overview.description: Project Showcase invites teams to present an AI-driven smart car prototype featuring road detection, traffic light recognition, and obstacle avoidance. The event highlights innovation in real-time intelligent systems, sensor integration.

rules: Each team must present their own original prototype and demonstrate its features live.
Teams must complete their demo within the allotted time and clearly explain the AI logic, sensor setup, and functionality.

title: Warangal Trading Ring
name: FinWiz Club

overview.description: Step into the world of high-stakes trading and test your financial instincts. Compete in a simulated stock market environment and prove your strategy under pressure.

rules: Each one will be allotted virtual stock of currency to trade.
The person with the highest portfolio value at the end will be declared the winner.

title: Startup Simulation Game
name: Innovation Garage

overview.description: Teams of startup founders begin with a random idea and compete by adapting to challenges across four 10-minute rounds. They conclude the simulation by delivering a 3-minute pitch on their evolved business concept.

rules: Teams must adapt their product idea within 10 mins per round.

title: Project Expo
name: Modelling and Prototype Club

overview.description: Project Expo is a platform for participants to showcase their innovative models and projects to an expert jury, gaining evaluation, recognition, and the visibility their work deserves

rules: Only original, participant-created projects are allowed; plagiarism will lead to disqualification.
Each team must present their project within the allotted time and answer the jury’s questions clearly.

title: StockFischer AI Chess Engine
name: Chess Committee

overview.description: The StockFischer AI Chess Engine event is a chess competition where participants challenge an AI chess engine. The event is expected to attract around 100 participants and will last approximately 2-3 hours, including an introduction and technical overview of the AI

rules: Each player gets a set amount of time for their moves; once a player's time runs out, they lose the game.
Participants will get a single chance to play a game against the Stock Fischer AI. The player who lasts the longest or wins will be the champion.

title: Code Red
name: HAM ARC Club

overview.description: Bomb Defusal Challenge, teams collaborate to solve riddles, decode messages, and defuse a 'bomb' within a time limit. It tests problem-solving, communication, and strategy skills.

rules: Teams must use only provided materials, no external help or devices allowed.
Bypassing stages leads to disqualification, judge’s decisions are final.

title: The Quick-Think Challenge
name: Modelling and Prototype Club

overview.description: Solve real-world problems specific to your branch, selected randomly, and craft a winning pitch within 30-45 minutes with access to full resources.

rules: Tackle branch-specific real-world problems selected randomly.
You have 30-45 minutes with full resources to craft your pitch.

title: SKY SCOUT
name: Science & Astronomy Club

overview.description: Build a functional telescope using a standardized DIY kit, promoting the practical understanding of optics and scientific curiosity. Creativity and accuracy will be evaluated.

rules: Participants must use only the provided DIY kits.
Telescopes must be completed within the allotted time without external tools or materials.

title: SDC GAMES
name: Software Development Club

overview.description: SDC Games is a thrilling survival themed event inspired by Squid game and Alice in Borderland. Players face intense. brain-bending challenges that test strategy,teamwork and quick decisions - delivering non stop excitement and adrenaline.

rules: Players must strictly follow the instructions of each game , any voilation will lead to disqualification
Teamwork and fairplay are essential , no external help is allowed
